description: Formal conservation law auditing for physics simulations

groundings.claim: Every continuous symmetry of a physical system corresponds to a conserved quantity

citation.author: E. Noether
citation.title: Invariante Variationsprobleme
citation.journal: Nachrichten von der Gesellschaft der Wissenschaften zu Göttingen, Mathematisch-Physikalische Klasse

code_paths: sim-laws/src/angular_momentum.rs — angular momentum from rotational symmetry
sim-laws/src/charge.rs — charge from U(1) gauge symmetry
sim-laws/src/symplectic.rs — Liouville's theorem from Hamiltonian structure

groundings.claim: Symplectic integrators exactly preserve a modified Hamiltonian, bounding energy error for exponentially long times
groundings.theorem: Backward Error Analysis for Symplectic Methods (Theorem IX.8.1)

citation.author: E. Hairer, C. Lubich, G. Wanner
citation.title: Geometric Numerical Integration: Structure-Preserving Algorithms for Ordinary Differential Equations
chapters: VI: Symplectic Integration of Hamiltonian Systems

groundings.claim: Splitting methods decompose Hamiltonian evolution into exactly solvable sub-flows

citation.author: R. I. McLachlan, G. R. W. Quispel
citation.title: Splitting Methods

groundings.claim: Higher-order symplectic integrators can be constructed by symmetric composition (triple-jump technique)

citation.author: H. Yoshida
citation.title: Construction of higher order symplectic integrators

code_paths: sim-integrator/src/yoshida.rs — Yoshida4, Yoshida6, Yoshida8 with exact coefficients

groundings.claim: Fourth-order symplectic integrators with optimized error constants exist (Forest-Ruth, PEFRL)

additional_citation.author: I. P. Omelyan, I. M. Mryglod, R. Folk
additional_citation.title: Optimized Forest-Ruth- and Suzuki-like algorithms for integration of motion in many-body systems

groundings.claim: Hamiltonian systems preserve the symplectic 2-form and phase-space volume (Liouville's theorem)
groundings.theorem: Liouville's Theorem on phase-space volume preservation

groundings.claim: CUSUM (Cumulative Sum) charts detect small persistent shifts in time-series data
groundings.theorem: Page's CUSUM procedure for change-point detection

groundings.claim: The PELT algorithm provides exact, linear-time change-point detection

citation.author: R. Killick, P. Fearnhead, I. A. Eckley
citation.title: Optimal Detection of Changepoints with a Linear Computational Cost
citation.journal: Journal of the American Statistical Association

groundings.claim: Discrete mechanics and variational integrators preserve discrete analogues of continuous symmetries
SHAKE and RATTLE algorithms enforce holonomic constraints while preserving symplecticity

citations.author: J.-P. Ryckaert, G. Ciccotti, H. J. C. Berendsen
citations.title: Numerical integration of the cartesian equations of motion of a system with constraints: molecular dynamics of n-alkanes

citations.author: H. C. Andersen
citations.title: Rattle: A 'velocity' version of the shake algorithm for molecular dynamics calculations

groundings.claim: Conservation violations in climate models can corrupt radiative balance and persist undetected for years

citation.author: S. Wan, M. Zhang, F. Raber, P. Sabin, M. van Lier-Walqui, S. Fridlind
citation.title: Identifying and correcting a systematic energy conservation error in a coupled Earth system model
citation.journal: Journal of Advances in Modeling Earth Systems (JAMES)

groundings.claim: Lyapunov exponents characterize the rate of divergence of nearby trajectories in dynamical systems

citation.author: G. Benettin, L. Galgani, A. Giorgilli, J.-M. Strelcyn
citation.title: Lyapunov Characteristic Exponents for smooth dynamical systems and for hamiltonian systems; A method for computing all of them

groundings.claim: KAM theory guarantees persistence of quasi-periodic orbits under small Hamiltonian perturbations

citations.author: A. N. Kolmogorov
citations.title: On conservation of conditionally periodic motions for a small change in Hamilton's function

citations.author: V. I. Arnold
citations.title: Proof of a theorem of A. N. Kolmogorov on the invariance of quasi-periodic motions under small perturbations of the Hamiltonian

groundings.claim: Kelvin's circulation theorem states that circulation is conserved along material contours in inviscid barotropic flow

citation.author: Lord Kelvin (W. Thomson)
citation.title: On Vortex Motion
citation.journal: Transactions of the Royal Society of Edinburgh

groundings.claim: The Boris algorithm preserves phase-space volume and energy in pure magnetic fields

citation.author: J. P. Boris
citation.title: Relativistic plasma simulation-optimization of a hybrid code
citation.booktitle: Proceedings of the Fourth Conference on Numerical Simulation of Plasmas

additional_citation.author: H. Qin, S. Zhang, J. Xiao, J. Liu, Y. Sun, W. M. Tang
additional_citation.title: Why is Boris algorithm so good?

groundings.claim: Gauss-Legendre Runge-Kutta methods are both symplectic and A-stable
Lennard-Jones and Morse potentials model interatomic interactions in molecular dynamics

citations.author: J. E. Lennard-Jones
citations.title: On the Determination of Molecular Fields
citations.journal: Proceedings of the Royal Society of London A

citations.author: P. M. Morse
citations.title: Diatomic Molecules According to the Wave Mechanics. II. Vibrational Levels

groundings.claim: Richardson extrapolation enables estimation of integrator convergence order from numerical data

citation.author: L. F. Richardson
citation.title: The Approximate Arithmetical Solution by Finite Differences of Physical Problems involving Differential Equations, with an Application to the Stresses in a Masonry Dam
citation.journal: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society of London A

GROMACS.description: Extracts energy terms from .edr files; plots total/kinetic/potential energy over time
GROMACS.limitations: Single-quantity monitoring only, no statistical detection, no change-point localization, no multi-law simultaneous auditing, no repair

citation.author: M. J. Abraham et al.
citation.title: GROMACS: High performance molecular simulations through multi-level parallelism from laptops to supercomputers

LAMMPS.description: Periodic thermodynamic output including temperature, pressure, energy; can compute per-atom and per-group quantities
LAMMPS.limitations: Threshold-based alerts only, no statistical analysis, no backward error analysis, limited to built-in quantities

citation.author: A. P. Thompson et al.
citation.title: LAMMPS - a flexible simulation tool for particle-based materials modeling at the atomic, meso, and continuum scales

MATLAB_ODE_Suite.tool: ode45, ode113, ode15s with event detection
MATLAB_ODE_Suite.description: Adaptive ODE solvers with local error control and event detection; mass matrix support for DAEs
MATLAB_ODE_Suite.limitations: Local error control only (not global conservation), no conservation-specific monitoring, no multi-law auditing, no repair strategies

citation.author: L. F. Shampine, M. W. Reichelt
citation.title: The MATLAB ODE Suite

ESMValTool.description: Earth System Model evaluation tool with conservation diagnostics for climate models
ESMValTool.limitations: Domain-specific to climate models, no general-purpose conservation auditing, no statistical change-point detection

niche: Formal conservation law auditing across MULTIPLE conservation quantities SIMULTANEOUSLY with statistical detection, change-point localization, backward error analysis, and automated repair — capabilities not available in any existing tool
